---
title: "Modeling"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
1 + 1
```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
#| echo: false
2 * 2

```

```{r}
install.packages("caret")
library(caret)
```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).

```{r}


Stock_Market_Dataset <- Stock_Market_Dataset %>%  select(Date,`S&P_500_Price`, Nasdaq_100_Price, Nasdaq_100_Vol., Microsoft_Price, Microsoft_Vol., Apple_Price, Apple_Vol., Google_Price, Google_Vol., Nvidia_Price, Nvidia_Vol.)

```

```{r}
full_crypto <- merge(crypto, Stock_Market_Dataset, by = "Date", all.x = TRUE)
```

```{r}

view(Stock_Market_Dataset)
head(Stock_Market_Dataset)


```

```{r}

head(full_crypto) 
```

```{r}
class(full_crypto)
```

```{r}

cols_to_check <- c("S&P_500_Price", "Nasdaq_100_Price", "Nasdaq_100_Vol.",
  "Microsoft_Price", "Microsoft_Vol.",
  "Apple_Price", "Apple_Vol.",
  "Google_Price", "Google_Vol.",
  "Nvidia_Price", "Nvidia_Vol."
)

# Keep only rows that are complete (no NA) in those columns
full_crypto <- full_crypto[complete.cases(full_crypto[ , cols_to_check]), ]

```

```{r}

#Split the dataset 80-20
set.seed(123)
full_crypto <- na.omit(full_crypto)
full_split <- full_crypto$Bit_Class_Binary %>% createDataPartition(p = .8, list = FALSE)

train_data = full_crypto[full_split, ]
test_data = full_crypto[-full_split, ]

```

```{r}

#Perform Linear Regression, list Correlations, analysis, significance, and testing
price_lm <- lm(Bitcoin_Price ~ ., data = train_data)

summary(price_lm)
```

```{r}



price_lm <- lm(Bitcoin_Price ~ Bitcoin_Vol. + Ethereum_Vol.+ Ethereum_Price +`S&P_500_Price` + Nasdaq_100_Price + Ethereum_Price + Apple_Price + Nvidia_Vol. + Google_Price + Google_Vol. + Eth_Classification, data = train_data)

summary(price_lm)
```

```{r}
# predicting on the test set
confint(price_lm)
```

```{r}
car::vif(price_lm)
```

```{r}
predictions <- price_lm %>% predict(test_data)

head(predictions)
```

```{r}
results_df <- data.frame(
  Actual = test_data$Bitcoin_Price,
  Predicted = predictions
)
head(results_df)

```

```{r}
results_df %>% ggplot(aes(x = Actual, y = Predicted)) +
  geom_point(color = "blue", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "Predicted vs Actual Values",
    x = "Actual Values",
    y = "Predicted Values"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
rmse_val <- RMSE(results_df$Predicted, results_df$Actual)
print(rmse_val)
```

```{r}
#Perform Logistic Regression on High/LowMid Binary Classification, Analyze and report results

class_model <- glm(Bit_Class_Binary ~ ., data = train_data, family = "binomial")
summary(class_model)
```

```{r}

install.packages("car")
library(car)
```

```{r}
car::vif(class_model)
```

```{r}
#Removing Collinearity
class_model_2 <- glm(Bit_Class_Binary ~ Bitcoin_Vol. + Ethereum_Vol. + Eth_Price_Level + Bit_Price_Level +
Eth_Classification + btc_weekly_return + btc_weekly_change +
Nasdaq_100_Vol. + Microsoft_Vol. + Apple_Vol. + Google_Vol. + Nvidia_Vol., data = train_data, family = "binomial")

summary(class_model_2)

```

```{r}
car::vif(class_model_2)
```

```{r}
class_model_sig <- glm(Bit_Class_Binary ~ Bitcoin_Vol. + Ethereum_Vol. + Eth_Classification + btc_weekly_return + btc_weekly_change + Google_Vol. + Nvidia_Vol., data = train_data, family = "binomial")

summary(class_model_sig)
```

```{r}
#low/mid = 0, high = 1 meaning prob above 50 is classified as a high class. 

predict_probs <- class_model_sig %>% predict(test_data, type = "response")

predict_class <- ifelse(predict_probs > .5, "1", "0")

predict_class <- predict_class %>% factor(levels = c(0,1))
```

```{r}
results_df <- data.frame(
  Actual = test_data$Bit_Class_Binary,  # replace with actual variable name
  Predicted_Prob = predict_probs,
  Predicted_Class = predict_class
)

set.seed(123)  # for reproducibility
sampled_results <- results_df[sample(nrow(results_df), size = 10), ]  # sample 10 rows
```

```{r}
sampled_results
```

```{r}
sample_n(results_df, size = 10)
```

```{r}
#calculate Accuracy
mean(predict_class == test_data$Bit_Class_Binary)
```

```{r}
train_data$Bit_Class_Binary <- factor(train_data$Bit_Class_Binary, levels = c(0, 1),  labels = c("Low", "High"))

test_data$Bit_Class_Binary <- factor(test_data$Bit_Class_Binary, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("Low", "High"))
```

```{r}
#SVM on Regression and Classification
set.seed(123)
svm_model <- train(Bit_Class_Binary ~ Bitcoin_Vol. + Ethereum_Vol. + Eth_Classification + btc_weekly_return + btc_weekly_change + Google_Vol. + Nvidia_Vol.,
                   data = train_data, method = "svmLinear", 
                   trControl = trainControl("cv", number = 15),
                   tuneGrid = expand.grid(C = seq(0.1,2,length = 20)),
                   preProcess = c("center", "scale")
                   )
plot(svm_model)
```

```{r}
summary(svm_model)
```

```{r}
predict_svm_class <- svm_model  %>% predict(test_data)
mean(predict_svm_class == test_data$Bit_Class_Binary)
```

```{r}
resample_summary <- svm_model$resample
boxplot(resample_summary$Accuracy, main = "Accuracy Across Folds")
```

```{r}
svm_model$results
```

```{r}

svm_radial_model <- train(Bit_Class_Binary ~ Bitcoin_Vol. + Ethereum_Vol. + Eth_Classification + btc_weekly_return + btc_weekly_change + Google_Vol. + Nvidia_Vol., data = train_data, 
                          method = "svmRadial", 
                          trControl = trainControl(method = "cv", number = 10),
                          tuneGrid = expand.grid(C =  2^(-2:2), sigma = 2^(-5:1)),
                          preProcess = c("center", "scale")
                          )
plot(svm_radial_model)

```

```{r}

predict_svm_class_radial <- svm_radial_model  %>% predict(test_data)

mean(predict_svm_class_radial == test_data$Bit_Class_Binary)
```

```{r}
#compare linear svm model vs Radial svm model vs multiple logistic Regression
#start with confusion matrix against each model
#compare accuracy results confusion matrix on training and testing sets.
#Get sensativity and specificity

confusionMatrix(predict_svm_class_radial, test_data$Bit_Class_Binary)


confusionMatrix(predict_svm_class, test_data$Bit_Class_Binary)


confusionMatrix(predict_class, test_data$Bit_Class_Binary)



```

```{r}

#best model is svm radial model

install.packages("pROC")
library(pROC)
```

```{r}
rad_roc <- roc(test_data$Bit_Class_Binary, predict_probs)
plot.roc(rad_roc, print.auc = TRUE)
```

```{r}
svm_model_radial <- train(
  Bit_Class_Binary ~ Bitcoin_Vol. + Ethereum_Vol. + Eth_Classification + btc_weekly_return + btc_weekly_change + Google_Vol. + Nvidia_Vol.,
  data = train_data,
  method = "svmRadial",
  trControl = trainControl(
    method = "cv",
    number = 10,
    classProbs = TRUE,             # <-- enable class probabilities
    summaryFunction = twoClassSummary
  ),
  metric = "ROC",                  # <-- use ROC to select best model
  preProcess = c("center", "scale"),
  tuneLength = 10
)

```

```{r}
predict_svm_prob_radial <- predict(svm_model_radial, newdata = test_data, type = "prob")
roc_result <- roc(test_data$Bit_Class_Binary, predict_svm_prob_radial[, "High"])  # adjust name if needed
```

```{r}
plot.roc(roc_result, print.auc = TRUE)
```

```{r}
write.csv(full_crypto,"merged_crypto.csv") 
```
